details_cut_bank_v1.description: Micro-intelligence for necklines, sleeves, hems, and cut details. Keeps advice flexible and confidence-first.

positive_neckline: That neckline really frames the look nicely.
This neckline choice feels clean and intentional.
The neckline opens the outfit up in a flattering way.
This neckline adds polish without extra effort.
The neckline works with the silhouette—very balanced.
This neckline gives a sharp, confident finish.
It feels modern and clean around the face/neck area.
That neckline is doing a lot of styling work—quietly.
This reads elegant because the lines are clean.
The neckline detail feels premium and put together.
positive_sleeves: The sleeve shape adds structure in a really good way.
That sleeve length is balancing the look nicely.
This sleeve detail adds personality without chaos.
The sleeves make the outfit feel more intentional.
This sleeve length looks very clean and wearable.
The sleeve fit keeps the overall line sharp.
This gives a nice proportion break—very chic.
The sleeve shape complements the silhouette well.
This sleeve choice makes it feel more styled.
positive_hems_and_lengths: That hem length is hitting at a really good point.
The length choice makes the silhouette look clean.
This proportion break feels intentional and balanced.
That length adds a nice vertical line.
This hemline makes the outfit feel polished.
The length works with the shoe choice nicely.
This reads premium because the lengths are aligned.
That hem gives a clean outline—no awkward cutting.
The proportions feel long and smooth here.
The length choice makes it feel effortlessly styled.
constructive_cut_feels_off: The cut is good, but the neckline might be fighting the vibe a little.
This sleeve shape is slightly competing with the rest of the silhouette.
The hem is hitting at an awkward point—so it breaks the line.
The neckline is a bit closed/heavy for the look you’re going for.
The sleeve length is slightly shortening the overall line.
This cut feels slightly boxy in one spot—not bad, just less defined.
The lengths aren’t fully syncing, so it feels a touch off.
The neckline detail is pulling focus more than needed.
The sleeve volume is adding bulk where you may not want it.
It’s close—just one cut/length tweak would make it click.
refinement_suggestions: If you open the neckline slightly (V/scoop/collar), it’ll feel lighter and more polished.
If you swap to a cleaner sleeve (less volume), the silhouette will read sharper.
If you adjust the hem (slightly shorter/longer), the proportions will look more intentional.
If you add a structured layer, it’ll clean up the neckline/shoulder line fast.
If you do one fitted element (neckline/sleeve/waist), the whole look looks more refined.
If you keep sleeves simple, you can go bolder on accessories—and vice versa.
Pair that hem with a sleeker shoe to keep the line long and clean.
If you want it more premium: clean neckline + tidy hem + minimal accessories.
If you want it more relaxed: softer neckline + easy sleeve + clean shoe.
Choose one detail to be the hero (neckline OR sleeves OR hem) and keep the rest calm.

usage_rules.default_pattern: Use 1 detail observation. Add 1 refinement only if user asks to tweak or says it feels off.
avoid: Over-personal claims about the user’s body.
Making it sound like a rulebook.
usage_rules.tone_integration: Reaction → Detail observation → Optional refinement → Invitation closer.
